message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Tac/Quila (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Jalisco-style Mexican / tequila & mezcal
Why it surfaced in discovery: Michelin Guide California 2022 and 2023 inclusion; consistent anchor on Palm Canyon best-of lists for elevated Mexican.
Discovery sources: https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/california/palm-springs/restaurant/tac-quila | https://tacquila.com/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/tac-quila/34614/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

# Tac/Quila Business Information

**Business Name:** Tac/Quila

**Location/Address:** 415 North Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs, CA 92262

**Hours:** 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Open daily, Sun-Sat)

**Cuisine Description:** "Authentic Mexican cuisine, cooked to perfection!" The establishment serves contemporary Mexican fare including street tacos, ceviche, enchiladas, and traditional desserts.

**Menu Structure:** 
- Food menu (available at /palm-springs-tac-quila-food-menu)
- Drinks menu featuring cocktails (available at /palm-springs-tac-quila-drink-menu)

**Ownership Information:** Founded by Liz and Mark, former lawyers who transitioned into hospitality

**Social Media Links:**
- Facebook: facebook.com/551626645324101
- Instagram: @tacquila_ps
- Yelp: yelp.com/biz/tac-quila-palm-springs

**Contact:** (760)-417-4471

**Notable Recognition:** Michelin-recognized restaurant (badges displayed for 2022-2025)

**Structured Data Signals:** OpenTable Diners' Choice badge present; website built on SpotHopper platform

Based on the search results, I can provide you with information about Tac/Quila's Michelin Guide recognition:

Tac/Quila was featured in both the MICHELIN Guide California 2022 and MICHELIN Guide California 2023. The restaurant earned its first major accolade in 2022 with inclusion in the Michelin Guide California as a recommended restaurant.

The restaurant is a Mexican establishment located in downtown Palm Springs that specializes in Jalisco-style cuisine. Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tac/Quila in Palm Springs:

## Review Ratings and Platforms

Tac/Quila combines gourmet Jalisco style cuisine with specialty tequilas and mezcals, and it has a strong presence on multiple review platforms:

**Yelp:** The restaurant has 4,418 photos and 2,942 reviews on Yelp.

**OpenTable/Google:** The restaurant has 3,005 reviews on OpenTable.

## Overall Reception

The restaurant receives overwhelmingly positive reviews across platforms. Matador highlights Tac/Quila for fresh ingredients, margarita flights, and vibrant Mexican dishes. Additionally, Tac/Quila is listed in the Michelin Guide, which is a significant endorsement of its quality.

Reviewers consistently praise the establishment for its romantic back garden and cozy patio seating, creative cocktails (margarita flights), and beautifully presented, elevated Mexican plates.

The restaurant appears to be a highly-rated and recommended destination in Palm Springs, known for its upscale Mexican dining experience and exceptional service.

2. **Clandestino** - An owner-founded eatery serving Jalisco-style Mexican small plates and hand-crafted cocktails in an intimate patio setting.

4. **Las Casuelas Terraza** - Since 1958, they have been serving four generations of Delgado family recipes to visitors and locals in Palm Springs. Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tac/Quila in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Tac/Quila is located at 415 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA and combines gourmet Jalisco style cuisine with specialty tequilas and mezcals. The restaurant is listed in the Michelin Guide.

## Perfect for Date Night
Tac/Quila is a strong date-night choice, with guests frequently calling out the romantic back garden and cozy patio seating, creative cocktails (margarita flights), and beautifully presented, elevated Mexican plates.

## Ambiance & Seating
The restaurant offers several seating areas including an indoor dining room, a lively front patio along the street, a quieter back garden/patio, and bar seating, with the back patio noted as charming and romantic. For the best experience, ask for a back patio or indoor table for a quieter, more intimate vibe and make a reservation by phone to secure the best spot.

## Menu & Cuisine
The sizeable menu offers both street tacos with typical fillings like carne asada or al pastor, as well as more upscale options like lobster and seared ahi tuna. The tortillas are made in house, the flavors are lively and the portions are generous.

## Reservations
Reservations are recommended due to the restaurant's popularity. You can call the restaurant at (760) 417-4471 to request seating preferences.

Based on the web search results, here are some of the best elevated dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2025:

## Fine Dining Establishments

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine and warm, welcoming atmosphere, offering a wide range of traditional Italian dishes prepared with authentic flavors.

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage offers a premier dining experience specializing in prime steaks and fresh seafood with floor-to-ceiling windows providing breathtaking valley views.

Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a French-inspired restaurant offering a refined menu with an outdoor dining area surrounded by lush greenery, perfect for special occasions.

El Dorado Polo Club in Indio offers American and Mexican dishes on an outdoor patio that delivers one of the most exquisite and unique dining experiences in the Coachella Valley.

## Contemporary American & Innovative Cuisine

Workshop Kitchen & Bar in Palm Springs is celebrated for its modern take on American classics and award-winning design.

Morgan's in the Desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing top local products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods.

For upscale dining reservations, Resy is the go-to platform for chef-driven restaurants in Coachella Valley, featuring buzziest new spots and high-end dining experiences.

Links: [{"title":"TAC/QUILA - Updated June 2026 - 4418 Photos & 2942 Reviews - 415 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Tapas/Small Plates - +17604174471 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/tac-quila-palm-springs"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmsprings.com/tac-quila/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila — Grokipedia","url":"https://grokipedia.com/page/tacquila"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://tacquila.com/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila – Palm Springs - a MICHELIN Guide Restaurant","url":"https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/california/palm-springs/restaurant/tac-quila"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - About","url":"https://tacquila.com/palm-springs-tac-quila-about"},{"title":"Tac/Quila | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/tac-quila/34614/"},{"title":"Dressed for the Part | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/tac-quila-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/tac-quila/547/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tac/Quila in Palm Springs:

**Opening Year:**
Tac/Quila opened in 2019.

**Owners:**
Tac/Quila is the brainchild of husband-and-wife duo Liz and Mark Ostoich, who have brought their passion for Mexican cuisine and culture to the heart of Palm Springs.

**History:**
The Ostoichs transitioned from careers in law to hospitality after opening their first restaurant, FARM, in downtown Palm Springs. They pursued their shared passion for Mexican flavors and hospitality. The Ostoichs collaborated closely with executive chef Alfredo Carrion, a Jalisco native and the third of four brothers who are all chefs.

**Recent Recognition:**
In 2025, Tac/Quila received recognition in the Michelin Guide, highlighting its continued excellence in cuisine and service.

The restaurant is located at 415 North Palm Canyon Drive in downtown Palm Springs and specializes in gourmet Jalisco-style cuisine paired with specialty tequilas and mezcals.

Links: [{"title":"Maleza Palm Springs | Modern Mexican Restaurant & Mezcal ...","url":"https://www.malezapalmsprings.co/"},{"title":"Dressed for the Part | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/tac-quila-palm-springs/"},{"title":"TAC/QUILA - Updated June 2026 - 4418 Photos & 2942 Reviews - 415 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Tapas/Small Plates - +17604174471 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/tac-quila-palm-springs"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - About","url":"https://tacquila.com/palm-springs-tac-quila-about"},{"title":"A Taste of Latin Flavors in Palm Springs: Discover Authentic Cuisine","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/palm-springs-mexican-restaurants/"},{"title":"El Jefe Desert Cantina","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/el-jefe-desert-cantina/929/"},{"title":"13 Must-Try Mexican Restaurants in Palm Springs and Beyond","url":"https://localemagazine.com/satisfy-your-taco-craving-at-these-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Maleza Palm Springs | Modern Mexican Restaurant & Mezcal Bar","url":"https://www.malezarestaurant.co/palm-springs"},{"title":"Tac/Quila","url":"https://casago.com/palm-springs/restaurants/tac-quila/"}]

Based on the search results, here are several excellent tequila and mezcal bars in Palm Springs specializing in Jalisco cuisine:

## Tac/Quila

Tac/Quila combines gourmet Jalisco style cuisine with specialty tequilas and mezcals. Located at 415 N. Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs, this restaurant features over 45 tequila and 16 mezcal options. The interiors are reminiscent of 1950s Mexico City with al fresco dining. Tacos with house-made tortillas are always a must, and the tequila and mezcal menu shouldn't be missed.

## Maleza Palm Springs

Maleza Palm Springs is a modern Mexican kitchen and mezcal bar serving bold flavors, craft cocktails, and a lively dining experience in downtown Palm Springs. It offers shareable Mexican favorites from Chef Ysaac Ramirez and a rare mezcal and tequila selection.

## El Jefe Desert Cantina

El Jefe has over 100 tequilas and mezcals behind the bar, with knowledgeable bartenders who can guide you to the perfect sip. El Jefe is located in the colorful lobby of The Saguaro Palm Springs and includes surf-and-turf taco varieties, plus veggie options.

## Las Casuelas Terraza

This family-owned, one of the oldest restaurants in Palm Springs, is known for its impressive tequila and mezcal selection with dozens of varieties to try, along with a choice of flights.

content.text: I have enough to assess. Let me check site structured data quickly.

input.description: Check Tac/Quila site for schema.org structured data

input.command: curl -s -A "Mozilla/5.0" https://tacquila.com/ -o /tmp/tq.html; wc -l /tmp/tq.html; grep -oiE "schema|ld\+json|itemtype|address|opening" /tmp/tq.html | sort | uniq -c
input.description: Inspect Tac/Quila HTML for structured data

content.text: No schema.org markup on the site. Now calling StructuredOutput.

input.name: Tac/Quila
input.corridor: downtown Palm Springs (North Palm Canyon Drive)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 2,900+ reviews; OpenTable 3,000+ reviews (combined 5,000+ across major platforms)
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (last 6-12 months) continue to praise the back patio/garden ambience, margarita flights, house-made tortillas, and elevated plates (lobster, ahi tuna); some mixed feedback typical of high-volume tourist-corridor restaurants but no signs of decline
reputation.longevity: Opened 2019 by husband-and-wife owners Liz and Mark Ostoich (former attorneys; also operate FARM in downtown Palm Springs); ownership stable through 2026
credentials: Michelin Guide recognition continued through 2024-2025 per restaurant's own badge display [UNVERIFIED — confirmed on site, not independently cross-checked on guide.michelin.com which returned 403]
Featured in Palm Springs Life ('Dressed for the Part')
Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs and Visit Palm Springs as a featured dining experience

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly on 'best Mexican restaurant Palm Springs' (top result, often #1), 'tequila mezcal bar Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs Mexican', and 'Michelin Palm Springs'. Does NOT reliably surface on broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' queries — those are dominated by Workshop Kitchen & Bar, Morgan's in the Desert, Le Vallauris, Chez Pierre, Edge Steakhouse.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When it surfaces, agent descriptions are accurate: Jalisco-style, gourmet/elevated Mexican, tequila and mezcal program, downtown Palm Springs location, Michelin Guide recognition. Some descriptions miscategorize it as 'Tapas/Small Plates' (Yelp's primary category) which understates the upscale Mexican identity.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: tacquila.com is built on the SpotHopper platform. No schema.org JSON-LD or microdata markup detected in the HTML. Hours, address, and menu links are present in plain HTML but not machine-readable as Restaurant schema. Menu is split across separate pages (/palm-springs-tac-quila-food-menu, /palm-springs-tac-quila-drink-menu) without structured MenuItem markup. This is a real gap for agent ingestion despite strong third-party signal.

competitors: Maleza Palm Springs (modern Mexican + mezcal bar, downtown)
El Jefe Desert Cantina (The Saguaro hotel, 100+ tequilas/mezcals)
Las Casuelas Terraza (legacy Delgado family Mexican, downtown)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — sits on the most-trafficked stretch of North Palm Canyon Drive and is consistently named as the elevated Mexican reference point for downtown Palm Springs
query_wins: upscale Mexican date night Palm Springs

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded site on SpotHopper (paid platform); active Instagram (@tacquila_ps) and Facebook; paid OpenTable presence with Diners' Choice; listed across Visit Greater Palm Springs, Visit Palm Springs, palmsprings.com, and aggregator sites (res-menu, pops-cafe). Palm Springs Life feature suggests PR investment. No agency footprint visible. Same ownership runs a second concept (FARM), indicating operator-level marketing capacity.
convertibility.reasoning: Tac/Quila is independently owned by reachable local operators (Liz and Mark Ostoich) with a demonstrated willingness to invest in marketing across paid platforms and earned media. They run a multi-concept operation in downtown Palm Springs, which signals both budget capacity and decision-making authority at the local level. The site has clear technical gaps (no schema.org markup, menus not structured for agent ingestion) which is exactly the kind of work an independent operator with marketing spend would pay to fix. Strong third-party signal (Michelin, 6,000+ aggregate reviews, Palm Springs Life feature) combined with weak first-party machine readability is the classic convertible profile.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Michelin Guide 2024 and 2025 inclusion — restaurant's own site displays badges for 2022-2025 but guide.michelin.com returned 403 so independent confirmation of 2024/2025 is not in hand. 2022 and 2023 inclusion is well-corroborated by Palm Springs Life and multiple secondary sources.
[UNVERIFIED] Exact opening month in 2019 — year confirmed by multiple sources, exact date not verified.
[UNVERIFIED] Specific claim of '45 tequilas / 16 mezcals' — sourced from a single secondary write-up (localemagazine), not cross-confirmed on the restaurant's own drinks menu page.
Yelp's primary category 'Tapas/Small Plates' is a categorization mismatch that may suppress visibility on 'Mexican restaurant' platform filters.
